mechanism_narrative: Sustained job growth, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in real retail and food services sales suggests weakening consumer demand and spending, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and profits, leading to a decline in S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If RSAFS declines significantly as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low and the Federal Funds Rate is rising, it signals an overheating economy and aggressive monetary tightening, which typically precedes a market correction as borrowing costs increase and growth slows.
failure_condition: If the specified conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of occurrences over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, strong housing starts indicate robust investment and consumer confidence, which translates into broader economic growth and positive equity market performance.
failure_condition: If HOUST shows consistent growth during an expansionary period, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative for more than two consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates a significant slowdown in manufacturing and overall economic activity, leading to reduced corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO contracts as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation (CPIAUCSL) allows central banks to maintain accommodative monetary policies, which supports higher asset valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL remains stable and low (e.g., below 2% annualized), but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative over any 1-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate coupled with declining retail sales indicates a severe weakening of consumer fundamentals and overall economic demand, which is highly detrimental to corporate profits and equity valuations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5pp over 3 months AND RSAFS 3-month change < -2%
failure_condition: If both UNRATE and RSAFS conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the Federal Funds Rate signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a month, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 2-year period.
